nts1.n: THE NTS BREAKFAST SHOW W/ FLO
nts1.d: Breakfast, in Flo motion - served by Flo Dill, every Monday to Thursday. In the top half of today's show, French-American group Userband are playing a couple of songs live in the studio, and then some MOTHS might get addressed if there's time.
Low, slow and sleazy sounds with babyschön, in the studio for the early afternoon shift…
An hour of cuts from the thriving Punjabi rap scene, featuring tracks from Sidhu Moose Wala, Simiran Kaur Dhadli, Shubh and more. Selected by @desisoundspace.
A monthly journey into H.O.U.S.E. hosted by Dan Beaumont & Nadia Ksaiba featuring interviews & mixes from super special guests. Expect two hours of old bangers and upfront pumpers.
Tune in, turn on, clock out. Sofie K brings sleazy synthpop, pumping proto-house and campy club cuts to the NTS studio once a month.

nts1.n: Chicken Foot Soup w/ Goya Gumbani and Dom P
nts1.d: London-based jazz artist Goya Gumbani stops by to share heartfelt picks from his collection.
Gqom king DJ Lag heats up the London studio with a salvo of new South African club sounds.
Weirdo beatmaking & low-slung club rhythms in an intense session from French producer Low Jack as he returns to the dancefloor with new release “Market”.

Drum & bass and jungle every month with Equinox & Dev/Null.
From the host: "Drum Poetry is back with an hour of freshness from Equinox and Dev/Null"
Moesha 13 is an artist and curator of Malian origin based in Marseille. She was unanimously selected as artist of the year 2020 on SHAPE Platform (Sound, Heterogeneous Art and Performance in Europe).

DJ, artist and techno impresario, Word of Command welds fevered collage and high-octane techno into a hallucinatory dreamscape. He is the founder of FIST (@funisstilltransgressive), a queer techno party that pays tribute to radical fun, while engaging the visibility of communities of otherness through techno in NYC.

nts1.n: THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ JACK ROLLO
The NTS Breakfast Show w/ ERROL AND ALEX RITA
SOUP TO NUTS W/ SCARY THINGS
WAYA FYAM W/ PRIESTE$$ TELZZZZZZ - Women in Ke HipHop
BASHMENT SOUND w/ Shadow Fyah - Rise & Toast (Live in Miami)

nts2.d: Take a Kokonut Trip with Tim Koh, playing vintage pop, kaleidoscopic rock & folk, and whatever else he feels.
Japanese musician and video game composer June Chikuma, known for her work on the Bomberman series, plays selections from her wide and diverse musical tastes in regular transmissions.

nts2.n: On the Road w/ Adelina S - Gris-gris tour pt. 1
nts2.d:
Traveler and DIY documentarian Adelina S traverses the savannahs and deserts of the Sahel region, recording griots who travel from remote village to village performing to make a living. Through storytelling and field recordings, Adelina shares the winding paths - and the choices - that led to growing close to and be welcomed by this community.
From the host: "Radio show featuring field recordings taken during a road trip with a Fulani Griot band performing for spiritual healers around Fouta-Toro region in Senegal. Through sounds and storytelling the show wil travel to the Sahelian Savanna where centuries old music tradition blends with amplified guitars."
The co-founder of the legendary Saxon Sound System, Lloyd 'Musclehead' Saxon steps up for a regular session, playing ska, reggae, rocksteady and beyond.
Explorations in music from Ruth Tafébé a.k.a The Afrosoulmessenger.

nts2.n: THE GREAT AFRICAN DISCO W/ TEMI KOGBE (R)
nts2.d: Lagos based collector and DJ Temi Kogbe traces connections in African popular culture through vinyl.

Alessio Natalizia is Not Waving. Take the plunge with his monthly hour of fucked-up left techno, leftfield electronics, brain-twisting oddball pop and post-punk, dub offshoots and other diversions. 
ecstaticrecordings.com
Oakland songwriter Maria BC selects an hour of tracks serving as inspiration for their new LP “Marathon”, featuring lo-fi pop, folk, ambient and more.

Malibu is a French electronic musician whose work sails between ambient and ethereal music.
Forever inspired by soft reverbed vocals and melodious chord progressions, Malibu’s music is an immersive nostalgic journey in a sea of synthetic strings and choirs.

Where the wall meets the floor, Temporal Cove is a body of sound exploring the ethereal and ephemeral. Smilegoth dives into shoegaze, post-rock, house and more to articulate a flow of reverberations that can be felt on every surface.
From the host: "smilegoth invites dear friends and Americana band Dutch Interior onto Temporal Cove for a cutty country and folk mix."
Sematary (fka Grave Man), a key figure in cult new wave drill and horrorcore collective Haunted Mound presents his monthly NTS Show.

Self proclaimed “Underground and Black”, Detroit native Ash Lauryn leads her musical charge with a candid love for the historical roots of dance music, and the power of reclamation in modern day America.
Cheeky slacker selections from everyone's favourite - Homeshake. Kick back.

nts2.n: NTS Guide to: Vietnamese Cloud Rap Vol. II (R)
nts2.d: A deep dive into scenes, record labels and genres from around the world.

nts2.n: THEY ARE GUTTING A BODY OF WATER (R)
Under Sleepy Moon w/ Yawning Portal (R)
VIBRATIONS FOR A BETTER WORLD W/ AOKI TAKAMASA (R)
CREEP ZONE W/ JAMES PANTS & MARC SCHALLER

subtle.n: Into The Lion's Den w/ Restrikt

subtle.d: Potent Funk records’ Matt Frost - Well known for his technical abilities and Sleazy bass music style, Frost covers all angles from UK alt Rap to the LA beat scene.

Get ready to embark on a captivating sonic journey of underground music with Charlie Kelly. Including new and timeless sounds from the depths of rap, drill, grime, and experimental genres from the vibrant streets of the UK and beyond. Charlie unearths the very best in music that pushes boundaries. Off the mic, Charlie is a Radio Producer and Social Media Executive with an eclectic music taste spanning across many different cultures, decades, and styles. Hailing from North London with a love for audio and pop culture, you can expect to hear tune after tune when you’re locked into Charlie Kelly on Subtle.
BirdE brings an hour of the freshest Drum and Bass covering everything from liquid to techy steppers - the deeper the better.

Originally from Cambridge, Counter Culture's selection deftly navigates across a myriad of influences and exclusives from himself & friends.
Expect some of the freshest rolling soul straight to your headspace.

subtle.n: Strictly Business Show w/ Qua Rush & Flipz MC
What's The Weather, Selecta? w/ Megan Wroe

subtle.d:
Hailing from Mumbai, now living in London, Chaitu is energised by the UK sound and scene.

With a love of all things weird, weighty and groovy; his mixes and productions bridge electro, techno, breaks, UKG and Jungle, with sprinklings of everything in between.

Chaitu champions provocative beats that break down the barriers of style, genre and expectation, all while keeping you moving.

In:flux Audio are a record label based in Leeds, UK founded by production duo Tik&Borrow. Since the label's inception in October 2013, they have dabbled in multiple dance music styles with the emphasis always being on the tracks being unique, and packed full of bass. Today the primary output for the label is from their Dubstep roster and this is focus of their show on Subtle - giving the artists and remixers chance to showcase their sounds with an hour guest mix, all hosted by label bossman Tik.

I’m a 19 year old DJ and producer from Newcastle upon Tyne, mainly playing and producing music around the 140bpm mark. I started out playing old 2-step and jungle records, before moving my focus to mainly dubstep and grime. Along with my friends Pirata and White Label, I run Newcastle’s only current 140 night, Well Irie.

subtle.n: THE CULTURE HEAD w. SAINT VOX
Skelecta presents: South London Dance Factory

subtle.d: With a monthly show on Subtle, Niceup is a London based DJ with a unique style. Pushing UKG, Breakbeat, Bass, Electro and 140. Weaving through genres showcasing new releases and older cuts.

Jedah started DJ'ing at the tender age of 18 years of age specialising in UK Garage/Grime. 

Jedah is currently the DJ for Grime Original Hollowman Jendor of the former Grime Collective Essentials/ OG's.

Responsible for “that massive grime tune with the Psycho strings”, UK producers/DJs Trends & Boylan are well versed in what the scene has to offer. They use this platform to showcase music from across the 140 spectrum from Grime to Dubstep. Anything goes!!

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy

dublab.n: Hannah Rubin and Noelle Armstrong – MELLOW DRAMA
Frankie Jax No Mad - Not Evil Radio
Daddy Differently and Dirty Dave - Things of Life
dublab.d: It’s the simple things isn’t it? The little things of life. Good food, sunshine, no traffic, dark coffee, 72˚ breeze, telephone off the hook and music every Friday morning a la Daddy Differently & Dirty Dave.

dublab.n: DJ Bird of Paradigm –  Paradigm Power Hour
Ill Camille x Jesse Fairfax - The Sweeter the Juice
Ryan Lee Moore – Holy Ghost Honky Tonk
Nia Michelle, Jordan Collins, Lewis Thompson – Channels
Echo Park Film Center - Optical Track
